title: FLS Module 5: Anesthesia and Patient Positioning

questions.text: Which laparoscopic procedure is most appropriate for use with local anesthesia and mild sedation in a carefully selected patient?

answerOptions.text: A) Laparoscopic appendectomy
B) Diagnostic laparoscopy
C) Laparoscopic cholecystectomy
D) Laparoscopic adrenalectomy

questions.explanation: Local anesthesia may be appropriate for limited procedures such as diagnostic laparoscopy, tubal ligation, or selected inguinal hernia repairs. More complex procedures generally require general anesthesia.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a commonly accepted benefit of general anesthesia during laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Improved control of ventilation
B) Enhanced neuromuscular relaxation
C) Fewer hemodynamic changes compared to local anesthesia
D) Greater flexibility in patient positioning

questions.explanation: General anesthesia is associated with more pronounced hemodynamic changes, particularly when combined with pneumoperitoneum, than local anesthesia.

questions.text: Which of the following is an important consideration when using a laryngeal mask airway (LMA) for laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) It is always preferred over endotracheal tubes
B) It prevents aspiration during positive pressure ventilation
C) It is controversial due to insufflation pressures and airway protection concerns
D) It is ideal for long procedures involving Trendelenburg position

questions.explanation: LMA use in laparoscopy is controversial because it may not adequately protect the airway during insufflation or prolonged positive pressure ventilation.

questions.text: What is the primary reason for using acid-reducing medications like H2 blockers or nonparticulate antacids preoperatively in laparoscopic cases?

answerOptions.text: A) Prevent postoperative nausea
B) Prevent aspiration pneumonitis
C) Reduce intraoperative bleeding
D) Minimize ileus

questions.explanation: Acid-reducing agents like H2 blockers or sodium citrate reduce the severity of lung injury in case of aspiration during anesthesia.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a correct positioning strategy to avoid brachial plexus injury in the lateral decubitus position?

answerOptions.text: A) Abduct the upper arm beyond 90°
B) Use pillows instead of rigid support
C) Place a roll under the dependent axilla and support the upper arm
D) Extend the lower arm fully beneath the patient

questions.explanation: A roll under the dependent axilla reduces compression, while the upper arm must be padded or supported in a sling to avoid stretch injury.

questions.text: When using a beanbag to maintain patient positioning, what is a key safety consideration?

answerOptions.text: A) Inflate the bag after final draping
B) Ensure edges extend past the midline for stability
C) Avoid rigid support in short procedures
D) Pad the surface to reduce pressure injuries

questions.explanation: A fully suctioned beanbag becomes rigid, increasing the risk of pressure-related injury. Padding minimizes this risk, especially during long procedures.

questions.text: Which position may require use of a footboard and thigh straps to prevent the patient from sliding on the table?

answerOptions.text: A) Supine
B) Steep reverse Trendelenburg
C) Lithotomy
D) Prone

questions.explanation: Gravity may cause patient slippage in steep reverse Trendelenburg, so secure restraints are essential to maintain safety and positioning.

questions.text: In the lithotomy position, which of the following helps reduce the risk of nerve injury and pressure complications?

answerOptions.text: A) Use of candy cane stirrups for all patients
B) Flexing knees fully to decrease hip angle
C) Using Allen stirrups or a split-leg table to conform to body shape
D) Tucking both arms to increase exposure

questions.explanation: These devices allow individual adjustment and reduce risk of overextension or compression, especially during long procedures.

questions.text: Which of the following positions may interfere with laparoscopic instrument handle movement if not properly set?

answerOptions.text: A) Reverse Trendelenburg
B) Lithotomy with knees higher than the torso
C) Supine with arms tucked
D) Left lateral decubitus with beanbag

questions.explanation: High knees may limit access to upper abdominal structures and obstruct handle manipulation during colon or omental dissection.

questions.text: What is the purpose of flexing the OR table when performing retroperitoneal laparoscopic procedures like nephrectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) To lower intra-abdominal pressure
B) To stabilize the diaphragm
C) To increase the distance between the costal margin and iliac crest
D) To reduce venous return and minimize bleeding

questions.explanation: Flexing the table creates more space in the flank, optimizing trocar placement and access to retroperitoneal structures.

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding arm positioning in upper abdominal laparoscopic procedures?

answerOptions.text: A) Arms should always be tucked to the sides
B) Arms may be abducted beyond 90° if padded
C) Arms may be abducted up to 90°, with careful padding
D) Arms are left on the chest to avoid stretch injuries

questions.explanation: Abduction beyond 90° risks brachial plexus injury. If left out to the side, the arms must be well padded and not exceed that angle.

questions.text: Which premedication is controversial in adults due to side effects such as dry mouth, despite its theoretical benefit in reducing bradyarrhythmias?

answerOptions.text: A) Glycopyrrolate
B) Atropine
C) Midazolam
D) H2 blockers

questions.explanation: While atropine can reduce bradycardia from pneumoperitoneum, its side effects in adults limit its routine use. It may be used intraoperatively if needed.

questions.text: A patient is positioned in a modified lateral decubitus position for laparoscopic adrenalectomy. What is a major advantage of this setup?

answerOptions.text: A) Facilitates bilateral port placement
B) Allows for rapid repositioning to supine if needed
C) Prevents injury to pelvic structures
D) Increases room for anesthesia access

questions.explanation: The modified lateral position enables table rotation to supine for specimen removal or emergency conversion to open surgery.

questions.text: What is the risk of placing the patient’s hands near a break in the OR table when arms are tucked?

answerOptions.text: A) Elbow hyperextension
B) Loss of vascular access
C) Pressure ulcers from shoulder supports
D) Crush injury if the table is flexed

questions.explanation: If the table bends during surgery, hands at the hinge can be injured. Tucked arms must be placed safely above or below the break.

questions.text: Which positioning accessory is especially helpful for splenectomy or nephrectomy in terms of maintaining optimal lateral angulation?

answerOptions.text: A) Candy cane stirrups
B) Trendelenburg footboard
C) Beanbag support
D) Arm sling

questions.explanation: A beanbag stabilizes the patient in lateral decubitus, critical for procedures involving the spleen, kidney, or adrenal gland.

questions.text: Epidural anesthesia may be used in laparoscopic surgery to:

answerOptions.text: A) Replace general anesthesia for all major cases
B) Increase insufflation tolerance in obese patients
C) Provide postoperative analgesia and reduce ileus
D) Eliminate need for muscle relaxation

questions.explanation: Epidural anesthesia, when used with or without general anesthesia, can improve pain control and shorten postoperative ileus duration.

questions.text: How should chronic medical medications (e.g., beta-blockers, antiepileptics) be managed preoperatively for laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Held 12 hours prior to surgery
B) Discontinued 3 days before surgery
C) Administered as per institutional guidelines
D) Replaced with IV equivalents in all cases

questions.explanation: Patients should continue chronic medications unless contraindicated, following standard protocols that apply to both laparoscopic and open surgery.

questions.text: Which of the following is recommended to minimize the risk of deep vein thrombosis in laparoscopic procedures?

answerOptions.text: A) Early ambulation alone
B) Routine use of diuretics
C) Sequential compression devices (SCDs)
D) Trendelenburg positioning

questions.explanation: SCDs are commonly used during laparoscopic procedures, especially when lithotomy or long operative times are involved.

questions.text: Why is a modified lateral decubitus position helpful for laparoscopic splenectomy or nephrectomy?

answerOptions.text: A) It limits diaphragm excursion
B) It prevents hypotension in supine patients
C) It offers exposure while allowing repositioning to supine
D) It increases renal blood flow

questions.explanation: This position provides optimal access and facilitates conversion to open surgery if needed, avoiding flank/subcostal incisions.

questions.text: When placing a patient in lithotomy for laparoscopy, what is a key positioning principle?

answerOptions.text: A) Fit the patient to the stirrups
B) Adjust positioning devices to the patient’s anatomy
C) Keep knees fully extended
D) Apply firm tourniquets to reduce bleeding

questions.explanation: For safety and comfort, equipment should conform to the patient's body—not the reverse.

questions.text: What feature of the operating room table is critical for optimal laparoscopic exposure?

answerOptions.text: A) Ability to rotate 360 degrees
B) Compatibility with robotic docking
C) Ability to tilt and flex in multiple directions
D) Fixed height platform for stability

questions.explanation: Laparoscopic procedures often require tilting or flexing the table (Trendelenburg, lateral tilt) to use gravity for organ retraction.

questions.text: In which position does gravity help retract small bowel out of the pelvis during lower abdominal laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) Supine
B) Reverse Trendelenburg
C) Trendelenburg
D) Lateral decubitus

questions.explanation: Tilting the patient head-down allows the bowel to fall cephalad, improving access to the pelvic organs.
